articles.title: Roblox’s daily users continue to drop as age checks slow growth
articles.description: Roblox's daily active users continued to slip last quarter due in part to its rollout of age checks on its platform. According to its latest earnings report, Roblox currently has 132 million daily active users globally, down from 144 million at the end of last year, which was a drop from 152 million in Q3 […]

articles.title: Congress keeps kicking surveillance reform down the road
articles.description: Congress has reauthorized Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act - but only for another 45 days. The extension is meant to give legislators more time to negotiate reforms to the controversial wiretapping bill. If the past few weeks are any indication of how future debates will go, however, we're in for a bumpy […]

articles.title: Apple’s iPhone revenue jumps to $57 billion despite chip shortages
articles.description: Apple's iPhone revenue jumped 22 percent to $57 billion over the past few months, despite supply chain issues impacting the device's processor. In an interview with Reuters, Apple CEO Tim Cook said iPhone "demand was off the charts," but there's "a little less flexibility at the moment for getting more parts." Cook added during an […]

Rivian held a ground breaking ceremony late last year. Now the company […]

articles.title: Show HN: Perfect Bluetooth MIDI for Windows
articles.description: Hi HN, I'm Erwin. I built a small free open-source utility that bridges Bluetooth LE MIDI keyboards into the new Windows MIDI Services stack so any DAW or Web MIDI app can use them as if they were wired. I bought a Roland FP-90X piano partly because it had Bluetooth MIDI. On my Windows 11 PC, pairing succeeded, but my DAW couldn't see the keyboard, and notes I sent from the PC never made the piano sing. After a regrettable number of evenings, I'd separated this into three independent bugs stacked on top of each other. The first one is the famous one: Windows only natively exposes BLE-MIDI thro

articles.title: Show HN: WhatCable, a tiny menu bar app for inspecting USB-C cables
articles.description: USB-C cables can be a mess. One cable charges at 5W, another does 100W and Thunderbolt 4, and they look identical in the drawer. WhatCable sits in your menu bar and reads the cable data your Mac already has access to. Plug in a cable and it tells you in plain English what it can actually do: charging wattage, data speed, display support, Thunderbolt, etc. Built in Swift/SwiftUI. Open source, free, no tracking. GitHub: https://github.com/darrylmorley/whatcable Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47972511 Points: 68 # Comments: 15

articles.title: 关于中年和中年危机的一些思考
articles.description: 关于「中年」这个极其宏大的话题，虽然可写的方面很多，但我不想把话题铺的太满。我写这篇文章本身的核心目的，不是为了说出些什么道理，而是只是想表达自己当下的一种人生处境，同时抛砖引玉，让更多的朋友去分享自己的中年收获和困境。只要有一瞬间觉得自己并不孤单，这篇文章的目的就达到了。 查看全文

tech.summary:
今日科技动态摘要

1. **AI与科技巨头法律纠纷**：马斯克与奥特曼的OpenAI庭审出现戏剧性转折，陪审团离席期间发生关键事件；马斯克在法庭陈述中暴露出七项重大失误。
2. **科技公司财报与生产调整**：苹果iPhone营收跃升至570亿美元，尽管面临芯片短缺；Rivian因特朗普政府削减贷款协议而缩小新电动车工厂规模，但R2车型投产推动营收增长。
3. **平台监管与安全挑战**：Roblox因年龄验证措施导致日活用户持续下降；Meta威胁若被要求进行“技术上不可行”的修改，将从新墨西哥州撤出应用；美国国会再次推迟监控改革。
4. **政治与科技交叉影响**：特朗普提名福克斯新闻医生为下一任卫生局局长；美国新闻自由排名跌至乌克兰之后，反映全球威权趋势；俄罗斯因太空港落入乌克兰打击范围而隐藏发射计划。
5. **重大网络安全事件**：Linux系统遭遇多年来最严重的威胁，全球措手不及；微软为所有Windows 11 PC推出Xbox模式；Housemarque提出新一代游戏开发新思路。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：马斯克与奥特曼的OpenAI庭审暴露出科技巨头间激烈博弈的内幕，同时Linux系统遭遇近年来最严重安全威胁，凸显全球数字基础设施的脆弱性。

ai.summary:
今日AI动态摘要

1. **AI评估（Evals）成为新算力瓶颈**：Hugging Face指出，随着模型规模增长，评估环节的计算消耗正成为制约AI发展的关键瓶颈。
2. **具身智能仿真框架开源**：新一代高吞吐、高保真并行渲染的具身智能仿真框架发布，旨在突破视觉仿真算力瓶颈，支持规模化训练。
3. **AI人才与财富关联研究**：对IMO/IOI奖牌得主的追踪显示，其成为亿万富翁的概率是普通人的1500倍，凸显顶尖AI人才的巨大经济价值。
4. **智能体与数字员工进展**：蚂蚁数科在以太坊全球基准评测中登顶，为智能体可信协作提供新方案；阿里发布数字员工产品QoderWake，可承担工程师、运营、销售等岗位。

**今日最值得关注的趋势是：AI发展重心正从模型训练转向评估与仿真等基础设施瓶颈的突破，同时智能体应用与人才价值加速向产业渗透。**

cctv.summary:
今日新闻联播动态摘要

1. 美国华盛顿特区一射击案嫌疑人在写给家人的信件中表达了对美国政府的怨恨，相关报道由《环球时报》等媒体披露。
2. 该事件反映出美国社会内部矛盾加剧，民众对政府治理的不满情绪上升。
3. 国际社会持续关注美国国内安全局势及政府应对措施。

今日最值得关注的发展是：美国国内枪支暴力与政府治理危机相互交织，再次暴露其深层社会矛盾。
